role.name: HR Executive
role.description: Evaluate people fairly through structured hiring decisions.

chapters.title: Interview Gone Wild
chapters.story: Candidate says, “I know Excel, Word, AI, Blockchain, Cloud, and little bit cooking.” Resume has 8 pages and 13 fonts.

choices.text: Hire immediately because resume looks heavy

outcome.title: Font-Based Hiring Ending
outcome.description: Candidate joins as accountant but starts making office posters in PowerPoint.
outcome.moralLesson: Fancy words do not prove real capability.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Fancy words do not prove real capability.

choices.text: Ask role-specific questions and verify real experience

outcome.title: Fair Hiring Ending
outcome.description: You identify strengths, gaps, salary fit, and role match. Candidate respects the process.
outcome.moralLesson: Good hiring is structured and fair.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Good hiring is structured and fair.

outcome.title: Budget Interview Ending
outcome.description: You know the salary but still have no idea what the candidate can actually do.
outcome.moralLesson: Hiring needs skill fit, not only budget fit.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Hiring needs skill fit, not only budget fit.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice interview scorecard workflow for a HR Executive situation involving hiring and role fit.
safetyGuardrails: Avoid discriminatory or biased hiring decisions.
Use structured criteria and job-related evidence.
Give respectful, legal-safe feedback without unsupported claims.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the interview scorecard workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: People-safety simulation: avoid bias, discrimination, or personal claims; use role-related evidence and legal-safe communication. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: People Ops Mentor says: At beginner level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the interview scorecard workflow before choosing speed.
chapters.safetyDisclaimer: People-safety simulation: avoid bias, discrimination, or personal claims; use role-related evidence and legal-safe communication.

guardrails: Avoid discriminatory or biased hiring decisions.
Use structured criteria and job-related evidence.
Give respectful, legal-safe feedback without unsupported claims.

chapters.title: Notice Period Negotiation
chapters.story: Candidate is good but has 60 days notice. Manager wants joining yesterday. Candidate wants remote, hike, and emotional stability.

outcome.title: Joining Mirage Ending
outcome.description: Manager prepares laptop. Candidate says current company has not released him. Laptop feels abandoned.
outcome.moralLesson: Hiring commitments must be realistic and confirmed.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Hiring commitments must be realistic and confirmed.

choices.text: Clarify notice, buyout options, availability, expectations, and manager priority

outcome.title: Clear Offer Ending
outcome.description: Everyone understands timeline and constraints. The offer process stays calm and professional.
outcome.moralLesson: Clear expectation setting prevents hiring friction.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Clear expectation setting prevents hiring friction.

outcome.title: Candidate Escape Ending
outcome.description: Candidate accepts another offer where HR did not sound like a deadline alarm.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.

chapters.title: Feedback Fireworks
chapters.story: A rejected candidate asks for feedback. Interview panel notes say “not fit” and one comment says “vibes unclear.”

outcome.title: Vibes Unclear Ending
outcome.description: Candidate forwards the mail. HR group chat becomes a legal awareness session.
outcome.moralLesson: Feedback must be professional, factual, and respectful.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Feedback must be professional, factual, and respectful.

choices.text: Share concise role-related feedback based on documented criteria

outcome.title: Professional Closure Ending
outcome.description: Candidate gets useful closure without confidential or careless comments being exposed.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.

outcome.title: Ghost HR Ending
outcome.description: Candidate posts “no response from HR.” Employer brand takes a tiny punch.
outcome.moralLesson: Candidate experience matters even after rejection.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use structured, role-related evidence and avoid bias or legally risky personal feedback.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Candidate experience matters even after rejection.
